source.title: Long Animation Frames API
source_claims: The API reports long animation frames that monopolize the UI thread.
The long-animation-frame entry threshold is greater than 50 ms.
Entries expose duration, blockingDuration, renderStart, styleAndLayoutStart, and script timing attribution.
The draft may change and does not imply W3C endorsement.

finding.observed: Long-animation-frame entries provide frame-correlated congestion evidence and rendering-phase timing that is closer to perceived jank than task-only timing.
finding.inferred: Mirror Cartographer can retain aggregate frame and rendering-pressure measurements while discarding script URLs, function names, DOM identifiers, and raw entries.
finding.proposed: Classify durations at or above 200 ms or blockingDuration at or above 150 ms as severe for internal triage. These severe thresholds are project policy, not specification thresholds.
finding.unresolved: Cross-browser support and field fidelity must be measured on each target device; unsupported observation cannot establish absence of congestion.

verification.method: isolated Node.js execution using byte-identical source and test content before commit
cases: known incomplete due to dropped entries
congestion observed with sensitive attribution discarded

falsification_route: Run the observer on target browsers and record supportedEntryTypes, callback count, and droppedEntriesCount.
Inject a controlled main-thread stall and require a corresponding long-animation-frame classification when supported.
Compare aggregate output with raw entries locally to verify that sensitive attribution is not serialized.
Reject bounded absence claims whenever support is absent, callback execution is unconfirmed, or dropped entries are nonzero.
limits: The primary source is an editor's draft and may change.
Deterministic tests validate classifier behavior, not browser implementation conformance.
The project severe thresholds are heuristic and require empirical calibration.
A complete bounded session does not prove absence outside that session.
